name: Analytical PyBullet Planner
description: Create an AnalyticalPyBulletPlanner that manages its own in-process PyBullet physics server: closed-form analytical IK paired with PyBullet collision checking, so IK solutions in collision are rejected. Pass a RobotCell and pick a solver.

inputParameters.name: robot_cell
inputParameters.description: RobotCell to plan against. Applied to the planner via set_robot_cell on every run.

inputParameters.name: solver
inputParameters.description: Name of the analytical solver: 'ur3', 'ur3e', 'ur5', 'ur5e', 'ur10', 'ur10e', 'staubli_tx260l', or 'abb_irb4600_40_255'. A dropdown is auto-created.

inputParameters.name: connection_type
inputParameters.description: 'direct' (headless, recommended on the canvas) or 'gui' (opens a separate PyBullet window for visualization, where a display is available). A dropdown is auto-created. Defaults to 'direct'.

outputParameters.name: planner
outputParameters.description: AnalyticalPyBulletPlanner with its robot cell loaded.
